settings.title: Five Years on the Corner
settings.excerpt: We opened Corkwell with eight stools and a borrowed fridge, and you kept the lights on.
settings.body:
This month marks five years since we opened Corkwell with eight bar stools, a secondhand fridge, and a list of twelve wines we could barely afford.

The room is bigger now and the list runs past a hundred, but the idea has not changed. We wanted a place where a glass of something good did not require an occasion.

How far the corner has come

Those first months were lean. We poured everything by the glass because we could not risk a bottle going unsold, and we learned the regulars by name because there were not many of us in the room at once. The secondhand fridge rattled so loudly we used to apologise for it. It finally gave out in year three, and honestly we were a little sad to see it go.

What grew was not just the list. It was the habit of dropping in midweek, of ordering the unfamiliar pour because we said it was good, of treating a Tuesday glass as something perfectly ordinary. That is the part we are proudest of, and it came from you, not us.

What has changed and what has not

  • The list grew from twelve wines to well past a hundred.
  • The room holds far more than eight stools these days.
  • The first pour still goes to people who treat good wine as everyday.

To say thank you, the first pour is on us all week for anyone who has been coming since the early days. You know who you are, and so do we. Mention the eight stools at the bar and we will know exactly what you mean.
